title: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amid Inflation Concerns
content: The Reserve Bank of India Monetary Policy Committee unanimously voted to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the eighth consecutive meeting. The central bank cited persistent food inflation driven by volatile vegetable prices and global commodity uncertainty as primary reasons. RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das stated that monetary policy would continue focusing on withdrawal of accommodation to bring headline inflation durably to the 4% target. The standing deposit facility rate remains at 6.25% and the marginal standing facility rate at 6.75%. Markets responded positively with the BSE Sensex gaining 350 points following the policy announcement.

title: India Retail Inflation Falls to 4.85% in March 2025
content: India's retail inflation based on the Consumer Price Index eased to 4.85% in March 2025 from 5.09% in February, driven by a moderation in food prices particularly vegetables and pulses. The Reserve Bank of India had projected inflation at around 4.5% for the fiscal year. Core inflation excluding food and fuel remained stable at 3.6%, indicating underlying price pressures are well contained. Rural inflation at 5.2% remained higher than urban inflation at 4.1% due to higher food weight in rural consumption baskets. Economists expect further easing of inflation in coming months if the monsoon is normal.

title: RBI Cuts GDP Growth Forecast to 6.6% for FY2025
content: The Reserve Bank of India revised India's GDP growth forecast downward to 6.6% for FY2025 from its earlier projection of 7.2%, citing global headwinds and subdued urban consumption. Despite the revision, India remains the fastest-growing major economy globally. The MPC noted that private investment recovery is gaining traction while government capital expenditure continues to provide fiscal support. The agricultural sector is expected to grow at 3.8% aided by a well-distributed southwest monsoon. Services exports and remittances continue to provide a cushion to the current account deficit.

title: GST Collections Surpass Rs 1.87 Lakh Crore in March 2025
content: India's Goods and Services Tax collections reached an all-time high of Rs 1.87 lakh crore in March 2025, registering a 12.6% year-on-year growth. This was driven by strong domestic consumption and improved compliance following GST portal upgrades. Finance Ministry officials attributed the buoyancy to better enforcement against fake invoicing and input tax credit fraud. IGST collections from imports contributed Rs 47,000 crore while domestic transactions contributed Rs 1.40 lakh crore. Analysts noted that sustained GST buoyancy above Rs 1.75 lakh crore monthly would provide fiscal comfort and reduce borrowing requirements.

title: Union Budget 2025 Focuses on Capex and Middle Class Relief
content: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman presented the Union Budget 2025-26 with a capital expenditure allocation of Rs 11.11 lakh crore, maintaining the government's infrastructure push. Significant income tax relief was announced with the new tax regime exempting income up to Rs 12 lakh per annum from tax. The fiscal deficit target was set at 4.4% of GDP indicating continued fiscal consolidation. Agriculture received a boost with PM Dhan-Dhanya Krishi Yojana announced for 100 low-productivity districts. Defence allocation increased by 9.5% to Rs 6.81 lakh crore reflecting geopolitical priorities.

title: India Foreign Exchange Reserves Hit 700 Billion Dollar Milestone
content: India's foreign exchange reserves crossed the 700 billion dollar mark for the first time, according to RBI data released this week. This provides import cover of approximately 11 months making India's external position one of the strongest among emerging markets. The reserves include foreign currency assets of 618 billion dollars, gold reserves of 67 billion dollars, SDRs and IMF reserve position making up the balance. Capital inflows from FDI and FPI investments as well as strong remittances have contributed to the build-up. RBI has been actively intervening in forex markets to prevent excessive rupee volatility.

title: Indian Startup Ecosystem Raises 14 Billion Dollars in 2024
content: India's startup ecosystem raised 14 billion dollars in funding during 2024, marking a recovery from the funding winter of 2022-23. Fintech and healthtech sectors led the funding rounds with several companies achieving unicorn status. The DPIIT registered over 1.4 lakh startups under the Startup India initiative, with Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Delhi NCR leading in startup density. Government schemes including Fund of Funds and Credit Guarantee provide crucial support to early-stage companies. India now hosts over 110 unicorns with a combined valuation exceeding 350 billion dollars.

title: India Signs 26 Billion Dollar Trade Deal with UAE
content: India and the UAE have expanded their Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement with new commitments worth 26 billion dollars in bilateral trade over the next three years. The deal covers digital trade, services, and green energy sectors with simplified customs procedures and mutual recognition of standards. Indian pharma exports, IT services, and gems and jewellery stand to benefit significantly. The bilateral trade currently stands at 83 billion dollars annually making UAE India's third largest trade partner. Officials expect the CEPA to generate approximately 500,000 jobs in both countries.

title: Indian Railways Sets Record Revenue of Rs 2.56 Lakh Crore
content: Indian Railways achieved a record revenue of Rs 2.56 lakh crore in FY2024-25 surpassing all previous benchmarks. Freight loading reached 1,702 million tonnes driven by coal, iron ore, and container traffic. Passenger revenue grew by 15% aided by increased footfall and dynamic pricing on premium trains. The Vande Bharat Express network now covers 100 routes across India with plans to introduce 50 more by end of 2025. Railway electrification reached 97% of the total broad-gauge network reducing dependence on diesel and cutting operational costs significantly.

title: SEBI Tightens F&O Trading Norms to Protect Retail Investors
content: The Securities and Exchange Board of India introduced stricter norms for futures and options trading following a study showing that 93% of retail traders lost money in derivatives. The minimum contract size was raised to Rs 15 lakh and weekly expiry contracts limited to one per exchange per week. Position limits for individual retail traders were reduced and mandatory risk disclosure requirements introduced. SEBI also mandated upfront collection of option premiums to prevent excessive leverage. The reforms are expected to reduce speculative activity and protect the estimated 12 million retail participants in the F&O segment.

title: India Wins ICC Champions Trophy 2025 Defeating Pakistan in Final
content: India clinched the ICC Champions Trophy 2025 title with a thrilling six-wicket victory over Pakistan in the final played at the Dubai International Cricket Stadium. Rohit Sharma led from the front with a captain's knock of 87 runs while Virat Kohli remained unbeaten on 63. Pakistan had set a target of 242 after posting a competitive total on a slow pitch. Jasprit Bumrah was the pick of Indian bowlers with figures of 4 for 38. This was India's third Champions Trophy title and the cricketing fraternity celebrated across the country with massive public gatherings.

title: IPL 2025 Season Kicks Off with Record Viewership on JioCinema
content: The Indian Premier League 2025 season commenced with Chennai Super Kings facing Mumbai Indians in a high-octane opener that drew a record 52 million concurrent viewers on JioCinema. The season features 10 teams competing across 74 matches with the final scheduled at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. Total IPL broadcast and digital rights are valued at Rs 48,000 crore for the 2023-27 cycle. New Impact Player rules have added strategic depth with teams deploying pinch-hitters and specialist spinners more freely. Ticket sales broke all previous records with some marquee matches selling out within minutes of availability.

title: Rohit Sharma Announces Retirement from Test Cricket
content: Indian cricket captain Rohit Sharma announced his retirement from Test cricket following the conclusion of the series against England, ending a distinguished red-ball career spanning 67 Tests with 4301 runs including 12 centuries. Rohit had been struggling with form and fitness in recent matches leading to widespread speculation about his future. The BCCI expressed gratitude for his contributions to Indian Test cricket particularly the memorable series win in Australia in 2020-21. Shubman Gill is widely expected to take over the Test captaincy. Rohit will continue playing T20 Internationals and ODIs ahead of the ICC T20 World Cup.

title: Neeraj Chopra Wins Diamond League Final with Season Best Throw
content: Javelin star Neeraj Chopra clinched the Diamond League final title in Zurich with a season-best throw of 89.49 metres, cementing his status as the world's premier javelin thrower. The Olympic gold medallist delivered a consistent series with four throws beyond 87 metres. Chopra attributed his performance to refined technique following guidance from his Czech coach Jan Zelezny. His preparation for the next Olympic Games in Los Angeles 2028 is reportedly on track. Athletics Federation of India expressed pride noting that Chopra has transformed javelin throw from an obscure event to a mainstream sport in India.

title: Indian Hockey Team Qualifies for 2028 Los Angeles Olympics
content: The Indian men's hockey team secured their berth for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics by winning the FIH Olympic Qualifier with dominant victories over New Zealand and South Korea. Harmanpreet Singh captained the side with exceptional defensive organization and quick counter-attacks. India scored 27 goals while conceding only 4 across six qualifying matches. The team aims to end India's long wait for an Olympic hockey gold that last came in 1980. Chief coach Craig Fulton praised the depth of the squad noting that competition for places drives standards higher.

title: Bajrang Punia Returns to Wrestling After Doping Clearance
content: Star Indian wrestler Bajrang Punia has been cleared by the National Anti-Doping Agency after the adverse finding was attributed to a contaminated supplement batch. Bajrang returned to competitive wrestling at the national championships finishing runner-up in the 65kg freestyle category. Wrestling Federation of India welcomed his return and confirmed his inclusion in the national training camp roster. Bajrang expressed relief and determination to reclaim his place at the top of world wrestling. His next competitive appearance is expected at the Asian Wrestling Championships in March.

title: Pro Kabaddi League Season 11 Draws Massive Crowds
content: Pro Kabaddi League Season 11 witnessed record attendance of 1.2 million spectators across all venues making it the most successful season in the league's history. Patna Pirates emerged as champions defeating Dabang Delhi in a pulsating final. Young raider Sachin Tanwar earned the title of Most Valuable Player with 287 raid points. The league has been instrumental in transforming kabaddi from a regional sport to a pan-India phenomenon with international players from South Korea and Iran boosting competition. The PKL broadcast deal is valued at Rs 3,000 crore for the next five seasons.

title: India Hosts FIFA U-17 Women World Cup Successfully
content: India successfully hosted the FIFA Under-17 Women's World Cup with matches played across six cities including Kolkata, Bhubaneswar, and Mumbai. Spain claimed the title defeating Germany in the final with a late extra-time goal. The tournament generated over Rs 800 crore in economic activity and attendance exceeded 3.5 lakh across all matches. The Indian under-17 women's team showed improvement despite not progressing beyond the group stage. AIFF announced a five-year grassroots development plan to leverage the hosting experience and build women's football infrastructure.

title: India Becomes Second Largest 5G Market with 120 Million Subscribers
content: India has overtaken the United States to become the world's second largest 5G market with 120 million subscribers, trailing only China. Reliance Jio and Airtel have together deployed over 250,000 5G base stations covering all major cities and district headquarters. Average 5G download speeds in India now exceed 200 Mbps making it competitive with global benchmarks. The government's spectrum auction and progressive telecom policy have been credited with enabling the rapid rollout. Enterprise 5G applications in manufacturing, healthcare, and smart cities are gaining traction with several pilot projects underway.

title: ISRO Successfully Launches Chandrayaan-4 Moon Mission
content: The Indian Space Research Organisation achieved a landmark success with the launch of Chandrayaan-4 aboard the LVM3 rocket from Sriharikota. The mission aims to demonstrate sample return capability by collecting lunar soil from the South Pole region and bringing it back to Earth. ISRO Chairman V. Narayanan described the mission as a major step toward India's Gaganyaan human spaceflight program. The mission incorporates indigenous cryogenic propulsion and advanced autonomous navigation systems. International collaboration with JAXA for the follow-up LUPEX rover mission is also progressing on schedule.

title: Digital India Programme Transforms Rural Financial Inclusion
content: The Digital India programme has facilitated 11 billion UPI transactions valued at Rs 18 lakh crore in March 2025 alone demonstrating the transformational impact of digital payments. Jan Dhan accounts have crossed 550 million with Rs 2.35 lakh crore in deposits reflecting the financial inclusion of previously unbanked populations. The government's Aadhaar-enabled payment system processes 50 million transactions daily in rural areas. Direct Benefit Transfer through digital channels has saved Rs 3.48 lakh crore in leakages since inception. India's digital public infrastructure has become a global template adopted or studied by over 50 countries.

title: IIT Bombay Develops AI Model to Predict Monsoon with 90% Accuracy
content: Researchers at IIT Bombay in collaboration with IMD have developed a deep learning model capable of predicting district-level monsoon rainfall 30 days in advance with 90% accuracy. The model integrates satellite data, ocean temperature readings, and historical precipitation patterns. Successful prediction of rainfall could help farmers plan crop selection and irrigation, potentially adding Rs 50,000 crore annually to agricultural GDP. The project received funding from DST and ICAR under the National Mission for Strategic Knowledge for Climate Change. Field trials in Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh, and Uttar Pradesh have yielded promising results.

title: India Mandates AI Governance Framework for High-Risk Applications
content: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology released India's AI Governance Framework requiring companies deploying AI in high-risk domains like healthcare, finance, and law enforcement to register with a government portal and conduct impact assessments. The framework follows the EU AI Act but adopts a risk-proportionate approach to avoid stifling innovation. Companies must implement explainability features for consequential automated decisions. A National AI Safety Institute has been established to conduct red-teaming and safety evaluations of foundation models. The framework applies to both Indian companies and foreign entities processing Indian citizens' data.

title: Tata Electronics Begins iPhone Manufacturing at Hosur Plant
content: Tata Electronics has commenced commercial iPhone production at its Hosur facility in Tamil Nadu making India only the second country after China to manufacture Apple's flagship product at scale. The plant currently employs 20,000 workers with plans to scale to 50,000. The production contributes to PLI scheme targets and is expected to generate exports of Rs 30,000 crore annually. Apple CEO Tim Cook visited the facility acknowledging India as a critical market and manufacturing hub. The Tata-Apple partnership is seen as a strategic step in diversifying global supply chains away from China.

title: India Launches National Quantum Mission with Rs 6000 Crore Outlay
content: The Government of India officially launched the National Quantum Mission with a Rs 6,003 crore investment over eight years to develop quantum computers with 50 to 1,000 physical qubits, quantum communication networks, and quantum sensing devices. Four Thematic Hubs will be established at IISc Bangalore, IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, and TIFR Mumbai. The mission aims to make India one of the top six nations in quantum technology by 2031. Indigenous quantum key distribution systems for secure government communications are a priority deliverable. Industry partners including TCS, Wipro, and Infosys have signed MoUs for application development.

title: Delhi Assembly Elections 2025: AAP Retains Power with Reduced Majority
content: The Aam Aadmi Party retained power in Delhi Assembly elections winning 42 of 70 seats, down from its 2020 tally of 62 but sufficient for a comfortable majority. Arvind Kejriwal was sworn in as Chief Minister for the fourth time. BJP made significant gains winning 26 seats marking its best performance in Delhi in over a decade. Voter turnout was 62.8%, higher than the 2020 election. Issues of pollution, water supply, and metro expansion dominated the campaign. AAP's governance record on education and health remained its strongest electoral asset despite corruption allegations.

title: Parliament Passes Digital Personal Data Protection Rules 2025
content: Both houses of Parliament passed the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules 2025 implementing the landmark DPDP Act giving Indian citizens rights to access, correct, and erase their personal data. The rules establish the Data Protection Board of India as the enforcement authority with powers to impose fines up to Rs 250 crore for violations. Companies must appoint Data Protection Officers and obtain explicit consent for processing children's data. Cross-border data transfers are permitted to countries on a whitelist maintained by the government. Industry bodies welcomed regulatory clarity while civil society organizations raised concerns about surveillance exceptions.

title: India-China Relations Normalise After Border Disengagement
content: India and China have restored patrolling rights along the Line of Actual Control following complete disengagement at Depsang Plains and Demchok, the last remaining friction points in Eastern Ladakh. Foreign Ministers of both countries held talks in Beijing marking the highest level bilateral engagement since 2020. Trade between the two countries is being cautiously reviewed with India maintaining restrictions on FDI from China. Border Infrastructure development in forward areas continues to be a strategic priority for India. Diplomatic analysts describe the normalization as pragmatic rather than a strategic reset in the relationship.

title: India Becomes Permanent Member of Expanded UNSC
content: After decades of advocacy, India secured a permanent seat on the reformed United Nations Security Council as part of a landmark expansion that added six new permanent members without veto power. Prime Minister Modi described the moment as fulfillment of India's rightful place in global governance reflecting its status as the world's most populous country and fifth largest economy. India's election to the permanent seat was supported by over 170 countries. Foreign policy experts see this as a significant diplomatic victory achieved through persistent engagement across G20, BRICS, and bilateral channels. India will push for reforms in global financial institutions from its new position.

title: Bihar Elections: NDA Retains Power with Comfortable Margin
content: The NDA coalition led by BJP retained power in Bihar with Nitish Kumar's JDU winning 121 seats in the 243-seat assembly. The alliance secured 172 seats in total against the INDIA bloc's 65 seats. Voter turnout of 57.3% was lower than the 2020 election. Development agenda centered on roads, drinking water, and electricity connectivity resonated with voters particularly in rural areas. Opposition criticized the alliance for lack of employment generation and rising crime. The result confirms the resilience of the Nitish Kumar brand despite multiple alliance shifts over the years.

title: Supreme Court Upholds Electoral Bond Scheme Unconstitutional
content: The Supreme Court in a unanimous five-judge bench decision struck down the Electoral Bond Scheme as unconstitutional holding that anonymous political funding violates the right to information of voters. The court directed the State Bank of India to provide complete data on bond purchasers and recipients to the Election Commission. Political parties that received bonds exceeding Rs 100 crore were directed to return funds to donors or deposit to the Consolidated Fund of India. The judgment called for a transparent alternative funding mechanism within six months. The decision is widely seen as a landmark ruling on electoral transparency.

title: India Implements Uniform Civil Code in Uttarakhand
content: Uttarakhand became the first state in India to implement the Uniform Civil Code governing matters of marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption uniformly across all religious communities. The UCC Committee chaired by Justice Ranjana Desai submitted a comprehensive draft that was cleared by the state cabinet and legislature. Critics from minority communities raised concerns about erosion of personal law protections while proponents argued for gender justice as the key benefit. The implementation is being watched as a model for potential national legislation. Legal challenges have been filed in the High Court challenging various provisions.

title: India-Canada Relations Improve After Diplomatic Reset
content: India and Canada have resumed normal diplomatic relations after a tense period marked by expulsion of diplomats over allegations related to Khalistani extremism. A newly elected Canadian government signalled willingness for a fresh start and both countries recalled their respective high commissioners to resume duties. Trade negotiations for the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement which were suspended have been reopened. The Indian student community in Canada which numbers over 300,000 welcomed the thaw as it eases visa processing delays. Intelligence sharing protocols have also been reactivated according to government sources.

title: AIIMS Delhi Performs First Robotic Liver Transplant in South Asia
content: All India Institute of Medical Sciences Delhi successfully performed the first fully robotic liver transplant in South Asia dramatically reducing recovery time from six weeks to under two weeks. The procedure was led by Dr. Subash Gupta and a team of nine specialists using the Da Vinci Xi robotic system. The minimally invasive approach reduces blood loss and post-operative complications significantly. AIIMS has applied to ICMR for approval to conduct a 50-patient clinical trial to validate outcomes. This positions India as a regional hub for advanced surgical procedures attracting medical tourists from across South and Southeast Asia.

title: India Eradicates Tuberculosis Three Years Ahead of WHO Target
content: India has achieved the milestone of tuberculosis elimination defined as fewer than 10 cases per lakh population, three years ahead of the 2025 Sustainable Development Goal target. The National TB Elimination Programme credited active case finding, Ni-kshay Mitra nutrition support, and new shorter treatment regimens with BPaL drug combination. Annual TB incidence fell from 210 per lakh in 2015 to below 10 per lakh in 2025. The success required treatment of over 2.8 million patients annually and engagement of 300,000 private healthcare providers. WHO Director General praised India's achievement as a model for other high-burden countries.

title: Ayushman Bharat Expanded to Cover 100 Million More Beneficiaries
content: The government expanded the Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana to cover all citizens above 70 years of age regardless of income, adding an estimated 60 million more beneficiaries to the world's largest government health insurance scheme. The health coverage amount was also raised from Rs 5 lakh to Rs 10 lakh per family per year for the expanded group. Private hospitals empanelled under the scheme increased to 28,000 ensuring wider access. Digital health IDs under Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission have been created for over 600 million citizens enabling seamless healthcare access. States are being urged to integrate their own health schemes with the national platform.

title: India Launches Phase 3 Trial for Indigenous mRNA COVID Booster
content: The Indian Council of Medical Research in collaboration with Gennova Biopharmaceuticals has initiated Phase 3 clinical trials for an Omicron-adapted mRNA COVID-19 booster vaccine developed entirely in India. The trial involves 40,000 participants across 22 sites in eight states. The vaccine demonstrated superior immunogenicity in Phase 2 trials with antibody titres three times higher than conventional vaccines. If successful this would make India one of only a handful of countries with indigenous mRNA vaccine manufacturing capability. DCGI approval for emergency use is anticipated by end of 2025 pending positive Phase 3 data.

title: New Delhi Records Worst Air Pollution in Decade During Winter
content: New Delhi recorded its worst air quality in a decade this November with PM2.5 levels reaching 987 micrograms per cubic metre against the safe limit of 60. Schools were shut for three weeks and flights diverted as thick smog reduced visibility to under 100 metres. The Supreme Court directed an immediate halt to construction activity and odd-even vehicle rationing. Punjab stubble burning recorded 5,200 fires in a single day contributing 38% to Delhi's pollution load. CPCB data showed that Delhi's AQI remained in the severe-plus category for 18 consecutive days an unprecedented stretch.

title: ICMR Study Links Ultra-Processed Food to Diabetes Surge in India
content: A landmark study by the Indian Council of Medical Research spanning 12 years and 45,000 participants found that daily consumption of ultra-processed foods increases the risk of developing type 2 diabetes by 67% in Indian adults. India currently has 101 million people living with diabetes making it the diabetic capital of the world. The study found that packaged snacks, sugary beverages, and instant noodles are the most harmful food categories. FSSAI has proposed mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling with traffic light indicators to help consumers make healthier choices. The findings will inform dietary guidelines under the National Nutrition Mission.

title: Mental Health Crisis Among Indian Youth Prompts Policy Action
content: Government data revealed that 14% of Indian adolescents aged 13-17 experience significant mental health disorders, prompting the health ministry to launch a National Programme for Mental Health of Adolescents. NIMHANS studies indicate that social media usage beyond three hours daily correlates strongly with depression and anxiety in youth. School counsellors will be mandated in all government schools under the programme with a training programme covering 50,000 teachers. NIMHANS has developed a culturally adapted cognitive behavioural therapy protocol in 12 Indian languages for digital delivery. Tele-MANAS helpline received over 2 million calls in 2024 demonstrating the scale of unmet need.

title: India Pledges Net Zero Emissions by 2070 at COP30
content: India reaffirmed its climate commitment at COP30 in Belem Brazil pledging to achieve net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2070 and to source 50% of electricity from non-fossil fuels by 2030 five years ahead of schedule. India announced a new Rs 8 lakh crore green energy transition fund to finance solar, wind, and green hydrogen projects. The Long-Term Low Emissions Development Strategy submitted to UNFCCC outlines a sector-by-sector decarbonisation roadmap. India called on developed nations to deliver the promised 100 billion dollar annual climate finance commitment. The government stated that India's per capita emissions remain well below the global average justifying development space.

title: India's Solar Power Capacity Crosses 200 GW Milestone
content: India's installed solar power capacity surpassed the 200 gigawatt milestone making it the third largest solar power generator in the world after China and the USA. Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Tamil Nadu collectively account for over 60% of installed capacity. The government's PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana has installed rooftop solar on over 10 million homes generating distributed energy and saving households Rs 15,000 annually on electricity bills. Grid integration of renewable energy is being enhanced through battery storage mandates and pumped hydro projects. India aims to reach 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

title: Severe Cyclone Fengal Causes Extensive Flooding in Tamil Nadu
content: Cyclone Fengal made landfall near Puducherry with wind speeds of 90 kmph causing severe flooding across Tamil Nadu and Puducherry. Cuddalore and Villupuram districts received over 400 mm of rainfall in 24 hours. Over 200,000 people were evacuated to relief camps ahead of the storm making it one of the largest pre-cyclone evacuations in the state's history. IMD's 72-hour advance warning enabled effective disaster preparedness with NDRF teams pre-positioned. Damage to crops and infrastructure is estimated at Rs 12,000 crore. State government announced a comprehensive relief package for affected farmers and small businesses.

title: India Achieves Forest Cover Increase of 2,261 Square Kilometres
content: The State of Forest Report 2023 released by Forest Survey of India recorded an increase of 2,261 square kilometres in India's forest and tree cover compared to the previous assessment. Total forest and tree cover now stands at 827,357 square kilometres representing 25.17% of the country's geographical area. Andhra Pradesh Chhattisgarh and Odisha showed the largest gains through active afforestation under the Green India Mission. However biodiversity experts cautioned that plantation monocultures counted as forest cover do not provide ecosystem services equivalent to natural forests. Western Ghats and Northeast India continue to face threats from encroachment and development projects.

title: Joshimath Land Subsidence Triggers Review of Himalayan Projects
content: Continuing land subsidence in Joshimath Uttarakhand with over 4,500 structures now showing cracks has prompted the Supreme Court to order an independent review of all major hydropower and road projects in ecologically sensitive Himalayan zones. Geological Survey of India attributed the subsidence to a combination of seismic activity, rapid construction, and poor drainage management. Over 850 families have been permanently relocated. The National Disaster Management Authority is developing a Himalayan town stability framework for 13 major cities in mountainous terrain. Experts called for stricter environmental impact assessment norms for infrastructure in fragile ecosystems.

title: National Education Policy Implementation Review Shows Mixed Results
content: A five-year review of National Education Policy 2020 implementation found that multidisciplinary education and the four-year undergraduate programme have been adopted by 623 universities while regional language medium instruction has seen slower progress. Student learning outcomes in foundational literacy and numeracy improved in 18 states under the NIPUN Bharat mission. However faculty shortages in STEM subjects and digital divide in rural areas remain significant barriers. Credit framework allowing flexible entry and exit points has increased enrollment retention. The review committee recommended enhanced funding for teacher training and district-level education management information systems.

title: IIT JEE Advanced 2025 Records Highest Ever Participation
content: IIT JEE Advanced 2025 saw a record 1.87 lakh candidates appearing across 219 examination centres nationwide. The exam was conducted in computer-based format for the first time across all centres. Vedant Pandya from Rajasthan topped the examination with 355 out of 360 marks. Female candidate participation crossed 30% for the first time reflecting the impact of scholarships and awareness programmes. IIT Council approved expanding total IIT intake by 5,000 seats across existing institutions. Results will be declared within three weeks and counselling through JoSAA will begin immediately thereafter.

title: PM SHRI Schools Programme Transforms 14,500 Government Schools
content: The PM Schools for Rising India programme has upgraded 14,500 government schools into model institutions with smart classrooms, well-equipped laboratories, sports facilities, and vocational training infrastructure. Annual assessments show that learning outcomes in PM SHRI schools are 23% higher than comparable non-programme schools. Digital content in regional languages has been made available through DIKSHA platform reducing the content gap between English and vernacular medium students. The programme has enrolled 3.4 million students and the government plans to expand to 30,000 schools in the next phase. Teacher professional development under the programme has trained 280,000 educators.

title: CUET Becomes Mandatory for All Central University Admissions
content: The University Grants Commission made the Common University Entrance Test mandatory for admissions to all 45 central universities and 193 deemed universities from academic year 2025-26. The standardised test replaces individual university entrance exams reducing stress on students applying to multiple institutions. CUET 2025 received applications from 14.8 lakh candidates across 13 subjects. Critics argue that the uniform test disadvantages students from state boards whose curricula differ from CBSE. UGC has set up a working group to review CUET question paper alignment with diverse state board syllabi and ensure equitable access.

title: India Ranks 39th in Global Innovation Index 2025
content: India improved its ranking to 39th position in the Global Innovation Index 2025 published by WIPO, up from 40th in 2024 and 81st in 2015, reflecting a decade of sustained improvement in research outputs, startup ecosystem, and technology adoption. India ranks first globally in ICT services exports and second in domestic market scale. Patent filings by Indian inventors grew 24% year-on-year reaching 75,000 applications. The report highlighted IIT Bombay and IISc Bangalore as world-class research institutions. India's innovation cluster around Bengaluru was ranked among Asia's top five innovation hubs for the third consecutive year.

title: भारतीय रिजर्व बैंक ने रेपो दर 6.5% पर स्थिर रखी
content: भारतीय रिजर्व बैंक की मौद्रिक नीति समिति ने लगातार आठवीं बार रेपो दर को 6.5% पर अपरिवर्तित रखने का निर्णय लिया है। खाद्य मुद्रास्फीति में निरंतर वृद्धि और वैश्विक आर्थिक अनिश्चितताओं को देखते हुए यह निर्णय लिया गया। आरबीआई गवर्नर ने कहा कि मौद्रिक नीति का उद्देश्य मुद्रास्फीति को 4% के लक्ष्य पर लाना है। बाजार में इस घोषणा के बाद सकारात्मक प्रतिक्रिया देखी गई। विश्लेषकों का अनुमान है कि यदि मुद्रास्फीति कम होती है तो वित्त वर्ष की दूसरी छमाही में ब्याज दर में कटौती संभव है।

title: भारत की खुदरा महंगाई दर मार्च 2025 में घटकर 4.85% हुई
content: उपभोक्ता मूल्य सूचकांक पर आधारित खुदरा महंगाई मार्च 2025 में घटकर 4.85% पर आ गई जो फरवरी में 5.09% थी। सब्जियों और दालों की कीमतों में नरमी मुख्य कारण रही। ग्रामीण महंगाई दर 5.2% रही जबकि शहरी महंगाई 4.1% दर्ज की गई। आरबीआई ने चालू वित्त वर्ष के लिए महंगाई का अनुमान 4.5% रखा था। अर्थशास्त्रियों का मानना है कि सामान्य मानसून के आने पर आगे भी महंगाई में कमी आ सकती है।

title: केंद्रीय बजट 2025 में मध्यम वर्ग को बड़ी राहत
content: वित्त मंत्री निर्मला सीतारमण ने केंद्रीय बजट 2025-26 पेश करते हुए नई कर व्यवस्था में 12 लाख रुपये तक की आय को करमुक्त करने की घोषणा की। पूंजीगत व्यय के लिए 11.11 लाख करोड़ रुपये आवंटित किए गए। राजकोषीय घाटे का लक्ष्य जीडीपी का 4.4% निर्धारित किया गया। कृषि और ग्रामीण विकास को भी विशेष प्राथमिकता दी गई। रक्षा बजट में 9.5% की वृद्धि के साथ 6.81 लाख करोड़ रुपये आवंटित किए गए जो सामरिक प्राथमिकताओं को दर्शाता है।

title: भारत ने आईसीसी चैंपियंस ट्रॉफी 2025 जीती
content: भारत ने दुबई में आईसीसी चैंपियंस ट्रॉफी 2025 के फाइनल में पाकिस्तान को 6 विकेट से हराकर खिताब जीता। रोहित शर्मा ने 87 रन की कप्तानी पारी खेली और विराट कोहली 63 रन पर नाबाद रहे। जसप्रीत बुमराह ने 4 विकेट लेकर पाकिस्तानी बल्लेबाजी को नियंत्रित किया। यह भारत की तीसरी चैंपियंस ट्रॉफी है और देश भर में जश्न का माहौल देखा गया। पाकिस्तान ने पहले बल्लेबाजी करते हुए 241 रन का लक्ष्य रखा था।

title: आईपीएल 2025 में रिकॉर्ड दर्शकों ने देखा मैच
content: इंडियन प्रीमियर लीग 2025 का आगाज चेन्नई सुपर किंग्स और मुंबई इंडियंस के बीच रोमांचक मुकाबले से हुआ जिसे जियोसिनेमा पर 5.2 करोड़ दर्शकों ने एक साथ देखा। इस सीजन में दस टीमें 74 मैच खेलेंगी। इंपैक्ट प्लेयर नियम के चलते टीमों की रणनीति में बड़ा बदलाव देखा जा रहा है। टिकटों की मांग रिकॉर्ड स्तर पर रही और कई प्रमुख मैचों के टिकट मिनटों में बिक गए। आईपीएल प्रसारण अधिकार 48,000 करोड़ रुपये में बिके हैं।

title: नीरज चोपड़ा ने डायमंड लीग फाइनल जीता
content: भारत के जेवलिन थ्रो स्टार नीरज चोपड़ा ने ज्यूरिख में डायमंड लीग फाइनल 89.49 मीटर के सीजन बेस्ट थ्रो के साथ जीता। ओलंपिक स्वर्ण पदक विजेता नीरज ने इस प्रतियोगिता में चार बार 87 मीटर से अधिक थ्रो किया। अपने चेक कोच यान ज़ेलेज़नी के मार्गदर्शन का श्रेय देते हुए नीरज ने 2028 लॉस एंजेलिस ओलंपिक के लिए अपनी तैयारी पर जोर दिया। भारतीय एथलेटिक्स महासंघ ने इस जीत पर खुशी जताते हुए कहा कि नीरज ने भारत में जेवलिन थ्रो को एक नई पहचान दी है।

title: एम्स दिल्ली में दक्षिण एशिया का पहला रोबोटिक लिवर ट्रांसप्लांट
content: अखिल भारतीय आयुर्विज्ञान संस्थान दिल्ली ने दक्षिण एशिया में पहला पूर्ण रोबोटिक लिवर ट्रांसप्लांट सफलतापूर्वक किया। इस प्रक्रिया से मरीज की रिकवरी समय छह सप्ताह से घटकर दो सप्ताह से भी कम हो गई। डॉ. सुबाश गुप्ता के नेतृत्व में नौ विशेषज्ञों की टीम ने दा विंची रोबोटिक प्रणाली का उपयोग किया। एम्स ने आईसीएमआर को 50 मरीजों पर क्लिनिकल परीक्षण के लिए आवेदन किया है। यह भारत को उन्नत चिकित्सा प्रक्रियाओं के लिए क्षेत्रीय केंद्र के रूप में स्थापित करता है।

title: भारत ने क्षय रोग उन्मूलन का लक्ष्य हासिल किया
content: भारत ने डब्ल्यूएचओ के 2025 के लक्ष्य से तीन साल पहले क्षय रोग उन्मूलन का मील का पत्थर हासिल किया। राष्ट्रीय टीबी उन्मूलन कार्यक्रम के तहत सक्रिय मामलों की खोज और निक्षय मित्र पोषण सहायता से 2015 में प्रति लाख 210 मामलों से घटकर 2025 में प्रति लाख 10 से कम मामले दर्ज हुए। इस सफलता के लिए 28 लाख से अधिक मरीजों का वार्षिक उपचार और तीन लाख निजी स्वास्थ्य सेवा प्रदाताओं की भागीदारी महत्वपूर्ण रही। विश्व स्वास्थ्य संगठन के महानिदेशक ने भारत की इस उपलब्धि की सराहना की।

title: युवाओं में मानसिक स्वास्थ्य संकट पर सरकार की कार्रवाई
content: सरकारी आंकड़ों के अनुसार 13 से 17 वर्ष आयु वर्ग के 14% भारतीय किशोर गंभीर मानसिक स्वास्थ्य विकारों से पीड़ित हैं। निम्हांस के अध्ययन से पता चला कि प्रतिदिन तीन घंटे से अधिक सोशल मीडिया उपयोग से अवसाद और चिंता का खतरा बढ़ जाता है। स्वास्थ्य मंत्रालय ने किशोर मानसिक स्वास्थ्य के लिए राष्ट्रीय कार्यक्रम शुरू किया। टेली-मानस हेल्पलाइन को 2024 में 20 लाख से अधिक कॉल आए। सरकारी स्कूलों में काउंसलर अनिवार्य करने की योजना बनाई जा रही है।

title: दिल्ली विधानसभा चुनाव 2025 में आप ने बहुमत बनाया
content: आम आदमी पार्टी ने दिल्ली विधानसभा चुनाव 2025 में 70 में से 42 सीटें जीतकर सरकार बनाई। अरविंद केजरीवाल चौथी बार मुख्यमंत्री बने। भाजपा ने 26 सीटें जीतकर अपना सर्वश्रेष्ठ प्रदर्शन किया। मतदान प्रतिशत 62.8% दर्ज किया गया। प्रदूषण जल आपूर्ति और मेट्रो विस्तार चुनाव के प्रमुख मुद्दे रहे। आप की शिक्षा और स्वास्थ्य क्षेत्र में कार्यप्रणाली मतदाताओं को आकर्षित करने में सफल रही।

title: भारत-चीन संबंधों में सुधार सीमा विवाद के बाद
content: भारत और चीन ने पूर्वी लद्दाख में देपसांग और देमचोक से पूर्ण सैन्य वापसी के बाद वास्तविक नियंत्रण रेखा पर गश्त के अधिकार बहाल किए। दोनों देशों के विदेश मंत्रियों ने बीजिंग में वार्ता की जो 2020 के बाद सर्वोच्च स्तरीय द्विपक्षीय संवाद था। व्यापार संबंधों की सावधानीपूर्वक समीक्षा की जा रही है। राजनयिक विश्लेषकों ने इसे रणनीतिक बदलाव की बजाय व्यावहारिक सामान्यीकरण कहा है। सीमा क्षेत्रों में बुनियादी ढांचा निर्माण भारत की प्राथमिकता बनी हुई है।

title: संसद ने डिजिटल व्यक्तिगत डेटा संरक्षण नियम 2025 पास किए
content: संसद के दोनों सदनों ने डिजिटल व्यक्तिगत डेटा संरक्षण नियम 2025 पारित किए। इससे भारतीय नागरिकों को अपना व्यक्तिगत डेटा एक्सेस करने सुधारने और हटाने का अधिकार मिला। डेटा संरक्षण बोर्ड को उल्लंघन पर 250 करोड़ रुपये तक का जुर्माना लगाने का अधिकार है। कंपनियों को डेटा संरक्षण अधिकारी नियुक्त करना होगा। नागरिक समाज संगठनों ने निगरानी अपवादों पर चिंता जताई है।

title: राष्ट्रीय शिक्षा नीति 2020 के कार्यान्वयन की समीक्षा
content: राष्ट्रीय शिक्षा नीति 2020 की पांच साल की समीक्षा में पाया गया कि 623 विश्वविद्यालयों ने बहुविषयक शिक्षा और चार वर्षीय स्नातक कार्यक्रम अपनाया है। निपुण भारत मिशन के तहत 18 राज्यों में बुनियादी साक्षरता और संख्या ज्ञान में सुधार हुआ है। ग्रामीण क्षेत्रों में डिजिटल विभाजन और स्टेम विषयों में शिक्षकों की कमी अभी भी बड़ी चुनौती है। समीक्षा समिति ने शिक्षक प्रशिक्षण हेतु बेहतर वित्त पोषण की सिफारिश की।

title: आईआईटी जेईई एडवांस 2025 में रिकॉर्ड भागीदारी
content: आईआईटी जेईई एडवांस 2025 में 1.87 लाख छात्रों ने परीक्षा दी जो अब तक की सर्वाधिक भागीदारी है। राजस्थान के वेदांत पांड्या ने 360 में से 355 अंक लेकर परीक्षा में शीर्ष स्थान हासिल किया। पहली बार सभी केंद्रों पर कंप्यूटर आधारित परीक्षा आयोजित की गई। छात्राओं की भागीदारी 30% से अधिक रही। आईआईटी परिषद ने मौजूदा संस्थानों में 5,000 अतिरिक्त सीटें बढ़ाने का निर्णय किया।

title: भारत वैश्विक नवाचार सूचकांक 2025 में 39वें स्थान पर
content: विश्व बौद्धिक संपदा संगठन के वैश्विक नवाचार सूचकांक 2025 में भारत 39वें स्थान पर पहुंचा जो 2015 में 81वें स्थान से एक दशक में उल्लेखनीय सुधार है। भारत आईसीटी सेवा निर्यात में विश्व में प्रथम और घरेलू बाजार पैमाने में द्वितीय स्थान पर है। भारतीय आविष्कारकों द्वारा पेटेंट आवेदनों में 24% की वृद्धि हुई। बेंगलुरू के नवाचार क्लस्टर को एशिया के शीर्ष पांच नवाचार केंद्रों में स्थान मिला।
